texto: The sublime can only be found in the great subjects. Poetry, history and philosophy all have the same object, and a very great object—Man and Nature. Philosophy describes and depicts Nature. Poetry paints and embellishes it. It also paints men, it aggrandizes them, it exaggerates them, it creates heroes and gods. History only depicts man, and paints him such as he is.
explicacao: Citação de Georges-Louis Leclerc sobre temas ligados a familia. “The sublime can only be found in the great subjects. Poetry, history and philosophy all have the …” articula familia, gods, heroes em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como heroes e history.

frase_original: The sublime can only be found in the great subjects. Poetry, history and philosophy all have the same object, and a very great object—Man and Nature. Philosophy describes and depicts Nature. Poetry paints and embellishes it. It also paints men, it aggrandizes them, it exaggerates them, it creates heroes and gods. History only depicts man, and paints him such as he is.
